title: Street Signals Strength Methodology
summary: Signal strength aggregates bounded per-signal contributions across institution class and initiative class. Each signal contribution combines source credibility, recency, and bounded source prevalence.

sourceCredibility.description: Weighted by source priority in sources.json.

recencyWeight.description: Exponential decay with a one-year decay constant, bounded between 0.6 and 1.0.

sourcePrevalenceWeight.description: Bounded multiplier that rewards source prevalence without allowing prolific sources to dominate matrix totals.

signalStrengthFormula.description: Per-signal contribution added into each relevant matrix cell.

aggregation.description: For each signal, add the per-signal contribution into the cell for its normalized institution_type and for each normalized initiative_type attached to the signal.

notes: The matrix displays aggregate strength, not raw signal count.
Signals tagged with multiple initiative types contribute to multiple columns.
The bounded prevalence term prevents large cells from exploding due to raw source volume multiplication.
